description.value: Event-driven Python framework for building multi-agent AI applications with layered Core/AgentChat/Extensions APIs and a Studio GUI

first-release.comment: Initial public release September/October 2023; v0.4 architectural redesign released January 2025.

latest-release.comment: Last tagged release python-v0.7.5 on 2025-09-30. Repo is in stated maintenance mode but still receives patches; most recent commit 2026-04-06.

license.comment: Code under MIT; documentation content under CC-BY-4.0.

covered-phases.comment: AutoGen provides orchestration primitives (teams, termination conditions, handoffs) but prescribes no spec/plan/implementation phases.

methodology-style.comment: Framework for building multi-agent systems; value is in the runtime and orchestration harness, not a prescribed methodology.

opinionated.comment: Teams, roles, and termination are user-defined; no mandatory workflow.

ceremony-level.comment: No required artifacts, reviews, or phase gates.

mechanization-level.comment: Users write Python code against the API; AutoGen Studio offers a no-code GUI for prototyping but production flows require code.

hitl-checkpoints.comment: HITL is optional via UserProxyAgent; not built into the default team lifecycle.

approval-gates.comment: No phase-level approval gates in the default configuration.

autonomous-e2e.comment: Teams can run to termination without any human interaction when UserProxyAgent is not included.

autonomy-level.comment: Default configuration runs fully autonomously; human feedback is opt-in.

rollback-support.comment: No built-in rollback; any undo mechanism is the user's responsibility.

supported-agents.comment: AutoGen is itself an agent runtime; it does not plug into Claude Code / Cursor / Codex. It integrates with LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Azure, Bedrock, Gemini, Ollama) rather than coding agents.

editor-integrations.comment: Primary interface is Python in a terminal/IDE; AutoGen Studio provides a web UI for prototyping.

model-agnostic.comment: Extensions include clients for OpenAI, Azure OpenAI, Anthropic, Bedrock, Gemini, Ollama, and others.

mcp-support.comment: McpWorkbench class integrates MCP servers via stdio, SSE, and HTTP.

artifact-formats.comment: AutoGen produces agent conversations and code; it does not generate spec/plan/task documents.

artifacts-covered.comment: No formal workflow artifacts produced by the framework itself.

project-layout.value: No prescribed layout; imported as Python packages (autogen-agentchat, autogen-core, autogen-ext).

artifacts-in-git.comment: No framework artifacts to track; user code is versioned normally.

language-agnostic.comment: Primary Python implementation; a .NET implementation exists but AgentChat is Python-first.

enforcement-mechanisms.comment: Agent behavior is shaped by system prompts and termination conditions; no hooks, linters, or CI gates built in.

memory-implementation.comment: Memory protocol with ListMemory (in-process), ChromaDBVectorMemory, RedisMemory, and Mem0Memory backends — spans session-only, vector-db, and external options.

persistent-memory.comment: Persistence supported when using ChromaDB persistent config, Redis, or Mem0; ListMemory is in-process only.

project-context-files.comment: No CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md convention. System messages and memory are configured in code.

cost-tracking.comment: OpenTelemetry spans record LLM call metadata including tokens; no built-in budget limits or per-phase roll-ups.

run-history.comment: Built-in OpenTelemetry tracing follows GenAI semantic conventions; works with any OTel backend.

telemetry-opt.comment: Telemetry is opt-in — users provide their own tracer provider; nothing is sent by default.

custom-templates.comment: No artifact templating system; system prompts are configured per agent in code.

hooks.comment: No formal pre/post-phase lifecycle hooks. Message handlers and termination conditions are the extension points.

sub-agents.comment: Users define custom agents by subclassing BaseChatAgent or configuring AssistantAgent with distinct system prompts and tools.

marketplace.comment: No first-party marketplace. Extensions API enables third-party packages distributed via PyPI.

install-mechanism.comment: pip install autogen-agentchat autogen-ext[openai]; autogenstudio for the GUI.

setup-time.comment: pip install plus configuring an LLM provider API key; first multi-agent example runs quickly.

self-hosted.comment: Runs entirely on user infrastructure; hosted LLM provider is required but no AutoGen service.

requires-account.comment: No AutoGen account required. An LLM provider API key is needed to actually call models.

git-worktree-support.comment: No explicit worktree integration; AutoGen is agent-framework level, not VCS-aware.

documentation-quality.comment: Comprehensive API reference, tutorials, and cookbook with runnable examples; some fragmentation across v0.2/v0.4 eras and ecosystem forks (AG2).

active-maintenance.comment: Most recent commit 2026-04-06 (maintenance-mode banner update); security fix 2026-03-26. Stated as maintenance mode with Microsoft Agent Framework as successor, but patches continue.

example-projects.comment: Cookbook, sample notebooks, and Magentic-One reference implementation are all public.

community-channel.comment: Discord via aka.ms/autogen-discord plus GitHub Discussions.
